nameMeanings.name: Ballarde
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English ballard ‘bald-headed man’ (compare Bald 3).
French: from the ancient Germanic personal name Balhard, composed of the elements bal, presumably meaning ‘torment, spitefulness’, and hard ‘hard, strong’. Compare Ballor .

variants.name: Ballon
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Sergio, Alfonso, Cesar, Conchita, Emigdio, Enrique, Felipe, Francisco, Jose Luis, Julio, Ruben.  Jewish Hyman, Isadore.

French and English (Middlesex): from the Old French personal name Ballon, Ballun, derived from the oblique stem of ancient Germanic Ballo, a short form of compound names based on the element bal, presumably meaning ‘torment, spitefulness’ (see Ballard ).

variants.name: Bald
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Inge, Karl-Heinz, Konrad, Kurt, Otto.
German: from the ancient Germanic personal name Baldo, or a short form of various compound names such as Baldwin , formed with the first element bald ‘bold, strong’.

Scottish and English: from the Norman personal name Bald(e), Baud(e), occasionally Bold, a short form of the common ancient Germanic personal names Baldwin or Baldric, or possibly a survival of Old English Beald (compare Balding ); the relevant element in each of these is b(e)ald ‘brave’. As a hereditary surname it seems to have died out in England but survives in Scotland.

variants.name: Ballor
meanings: Altered form of French Ballard 2, reflecting the characteristic Canadian and American French rounding of the -ard ending. It is found mainly in MI.

variants.name: Bollard
meanings: English (Northamptonshire) and Irish: probably a variant of   Ballard  . According to MacLysaght, this is a surname of Dutch origin which was taken to Ireland early in the 18th century.
French: from the ancient Germanic personal name Bolohard, composed of the elements bolo ‘friend, brother’ and hard ‘hard, strong’.

nameMeanings.name: Annes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: and English: a medieval vernacular form of   Agnes  , which gave rise to a surname. Its modern use as a given name is probably at least in part a transferred use of the surname as well as a revival of the medieval given name.

variants.name: Annice
meanings: Variant spelling of Annis , based on the numerous women's names ending in -ice.

variants.name: Annys
meanings: and English: a medieval vernacular form of   Agnes  , which gave rise to a surname. Its modern use as a given name is probably at least in part a transferred use of the surname as well as a revival of the medieval given name.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

en.lifeSummary: Annes Ballarde was born in 1499, in Tanworth in Arden, Warwickshire, England. She had at least 4 sons and 2 daughters with Robert Smyth. She died on 29 August 1600, in Ely, Cambridgeshire, England, at the age of 101, and was buried in Ely, Cambridgeshire, England.

ko.lifeSummary: Annes Ballarde(은)는 1499년 영국 워윅실르 탠워스 인 아든에서 태어났다. 그녀는 Robert Smyth(와)과 적어도 네 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1600년 8월 29일 영국 캄브리지실르 엘리에서 101세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 캄브리지실르 엘리에 묻혔다.
